mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that suppress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median forward returns, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% quarter-over-quarter during an economic expansion phase reflects robust manufacturing output that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive forward market returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during expansion phases, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50% when GDP growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening employment conditions that boost aggregate demand and corporate revenue forecasts, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll increases correlate with negative forward returns in over 55% of observations, or rank correlation remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase surpassing 0.4% triggers inflation concerns that force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling unemployment signals an overheating economy where labor shortages constrain output growth, prompting negative equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Divergence periods show positive median returns, or the interaction effect fails to improve predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects credit constraints on manufacturing that erode earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods yield positive forward returns, or the regime filter reduces out-of-sample hit rate below 45%.
